_meta.notes: Race × faction interactions. The four factions (choristers / rust_hand / idle / forsaken) cross-cut the five races. This file maps which factions dominate which regions, where the tensions sit, and which sub-populations have unusual cross-cutting allegiances.

highfield-ascending.rationale: the long song fits the dwarven sky-bound rhythm; cider tastings frame faction meetings.

saltgale.rationale: elven Branchmen finance Rust Hand iron contracts; the act is profitable on any side.

the-coral-anchorage.rationale: the halfling navy moves Rust Hand iron up and down the coast; some captains are Forsaken in private.

the-long-indices.rationale: orcish reluctance to take sides reads as Idle; some Hush-readers retire into Forsaken silence.

threadwarden.rationale: Coldhammers ally with Rust Hand; weavers and Pageborn tend toward Choristers.

caelum-by-the-wash.rationale: the metropolis houses chapters of all four. faction tensions are negotiated in public houses near the Spoke Square.

tensions.summary: the Choristers sing; the Forsaken refuse to. Choristers consider this rude; the Forsaken consider it the point.
the Rust Hand traffics in iron; the dwarves consider mining deviant. Highfield Topgrove houses refuse to host Rust Hand emissaries.
the Idle take no side, which everyone reads as a side. Quietmile is mostly Idle; nobody finds this restful.
the halfling navy will take Rust Hand iron contracts but refuses Chorister missionary contracts. The reasons are written in a four-ribbon ledger.

crossCuttingSubPopulations.summary: elven contract scribes write the paper for both — and sometimes for both sides of the same deal.
orcish commentators refuse formal alignment but consistently annotate Forsaken texts with Idle marginalia.
the halfling search-and-rescue caste skews Forsaken — they have seen what comes when the choir is too loud.
the only human sub-population that learns orcish; the Choristers value paper-keepers.
